messages.content: Greeted user, loaded mind-context, displayed reduced mindmap (session/work/documentation nodes). Fresh session with no prior summaries.
User pointed out I didn't actually read mind_memory.md or apply conventions (theme, layout) when displaying the mindmap at session start. Just echoed a bare skeleton.

Acknowledged the gap. Read mind_memory.md and conventions.json. Identified missed conventions: theme header (conv-002), layout half/half (conv-001), mind-first read principle. Will properly read and apply conventions on every mindmap display going forward.
Can we make sure conventions are read and applied at start/resume/compaction before printing the mindmap?

Updated SKILL.md: added auto-load of mindmap conventions from conventions.json, changed mandatory output rule to explicit 5-step sequence (READ conventions, APPLY them, OUTPUT mindmap with theme, summaries, confirmation). Conventions are now loaded as data not just referenced in instructions.
Can the mindmap (full) be used by Claude as a memory grid to read and apply? That is the purpose of this project.

Confirmed: the mindmap IS the memory grid — every node is a behavior directive. Identified gap: skill instructions focus on displaying, not on applying nodes as operational rules. Proposed strengthening the skill with explicit 'memory grid' framing where each node group (architecture, conventions, constraints, work, session) maps to a behavioral category.
